title: What Is AI?
description: What artificial intelligence is, how rule-based and learning-based approaches differ, and how to recognise them in everyday systems.
objectives: Define artificial intelligence as computational systems that perform tasks which would normally require human intelligence
Distinguish narrow (weak) AI, which does one task, from the aspirational idea of general (strong) AI, which does not yet exist
Contrast rule-based (symbolic) AI, built from explicit hand-coded rules, with learning-based AI, which improves from data
Explain the Turing test as one way of assessing 'intelligent' behaviour, and describe its key limitations
Classify everyday AI systems, such as recommendation engines, voice assistants, image recognition and game-playing programs, by which paradigm they primarily use

lessons.title: What counts as AI? Narrow vs general intelligence
The Turing test and real-world AI
